bugs.error_message: https://patelrep-web.vercel.app/dashboard?unauthorized=%2Fdashboard is showing The page isn't redirecting properly
bugs.root_cause: The proxy redirected users with an authenticated session but no resolved app role from /dashboard back to /dashboard?unauthorized=/dashboard, creating a self-redirect loop.
bugs.fix: Extracted proxy route decisions to apps/web/lib/utils/routeGuard.ts, allowed /dashboard as the safe fallback for unresolved app roles, kept restricted modules role-gated, and stripped stale redirect query params when redirecting.

bugs.error_message: npm run type-check initially failed with missing react/next modules; npm install --no-package-lock timed out on first attempt but left dependencies usable
bugs.root_cause: The local apps/web dependency install was partial and lacked runtime packages such as react and next.
bugs.fix: Ran npm install --no-package-lock in apps/web, then reran type-check, lint, and build successfully.

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling Ã¢â‚¬â€ exceptions would propagate uncaught

bugs.error_message: Call to function 'NativeDatabase.execAsync' has been rejected. Ã¢â€ â€™ Caused by: near "EXISTS": syntax error
bugs.root_cause: ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS is PostgreSQL syntax; SQLite does not support IF NOT EXISTS on ALTER TABLE. execAsync passed this invalid syntax inside a multi-statement string.
bugs.fix: Moved ALTER TABLE statements out of execAsync into individual db.runAsync calls wrapped in try/catch so duplicate-column errors (column already exists) are silently ignored Ã¢â‚¬â€ the SQLite-compatible migration pattern.

bugs.error_message: After logging in on https://patelrep-web.vercel.app/login?redirectTo=%2Fdashboard it redirects to https://patelrep-web.vercel.app/dashboard?unauthorized=%2Fdashboard
bugs.root_cause: The proxy treated the Supabase top-level database role claim (`authenticated`) as a PatelRep app role because it cast `jwtClaims.role` directly to UserRole. That made `/dashboard` fail RBAC after login and redirect to itself with `unauthorized=/dashboard`.
bugs.fix: Added `toAppRole()` in apps/web/lib/utils/routeGuard.ts to whitelist only PatelRep app roles, used it in proxy role extraction, added a regression test for Supabase DB roles, deployed to Vercel production, and verified login reaches /dashboard with /v1/auth/me 200.

bugs.root_cause: borderColor: C.line, Ã¢â€ â€™ C.accent,; backgroundColor: C.surface, Ã¢â€ â€™ C.accent,
bugs.fix: Changed borderColor: C.line, Ã¢â€ â€™ C.accent,; backgroundColor: C.surface, Ã¢â€ â€™ C.accent,

bugs.error_message: when i click any tab it stays on the dashbaord it does not go to that tab. the url changes but it still shows the dashboard
bugs.root_cause: The proxy only trusted JWT `role`/user metadata roles. Supabase sessions expose the top-level database role `authenticated`, while the PatelRep app role can be in the custom JWT `user_role` claim and is also hydrated from /auth/me after login. The sidebar could show app-role tabs client-side while server route checks still treated the user as unresolved and bounced restricted routes back to /dashboard.
bugs.fix: Added getAppRoleFromSources(), read the custom JWT user_role claim before the database role, wrote a non-secret pr_role cookie when /auth/me or auth events resolve the app role, cleared it on sign-out/session expiry, and let the proxy use that cookie as the final app-role source for UI route gating.

bugs.error_message: PowerShell reported `dashboard : The term 'dashboard' is not recognized` when reading apps/web/app/(dashboard) paths
bugs.root_cause: App Router route-group parentheses were passed to PowerShell without a literal path, so PowerShell interpreted `(dashboard)` as an expression.
bugs.fix: Used quoted paths with -LiteralPath for Get-Content and quoted path arguments for rg.

bugs.root_cause: This checkout's root package.json does not expose npm workspaces, so workspace-targeted npm commands fail before running the web app scripts.
bugs.fix: Ran the web verification commands from apps/web with npm run type-check, npm run lint, and npm run build.

bugs.error_message: EAS Android production build failed: Could not resolve project :react-native-async-storage_async-storage / :react-native-community_netinfo / :react-native-safe-area-context / :react-native-screens; No matching variant; No variants exist.
bugs.root_cause: EAS archived the checked-in native apps/mobile/android folder, so it skipped Expo prebuild and used stale native autolinking state. The archive also included tracked .expo local state, and Expo Router was one patch behind SDK 54's expected version.
bugs.fix: Excluded apps/mobile/android and apps/mobile/.expo from the EAS archive, untracked generated .expo files, upgraded expo-router to ~6.0.24, and verified a temp archive can run expo prebuild to generate android/settings.gradle and android/app/build.gradle.

bugs.error_message: Failed to refresh rooms: Call to function 'NativeDatabase.execAsync' has been rejected. Ã¢â€ â€™ Caused by: cannot rollback - no transaction is active
bugs.root_cause: Race condition: NetInfo fires isConnected=true multiple times on Android; syncOnConnect() is fire-and-forget (no await) in the listener. Two concurrent withTransactionAsync calls on the same SQLite connection: the second BEGIN fails ('cannot start transaction within transaction'), its catch calls ROLLBACK which silently kills the first call's transaction, then the first call's own ROLLBACK fails with 'cannot rollback - no transaction is active'. Secondary: getDb() set _db before initSchema() finished, so concurrent callers got a partially-initialized DB.
bugs.fix: (1) Added _syncOnConnectInProgress guard to syncOnConnect() so concurrent calls bail early. (2) Replaced _db singleton with a promise-mutex (_dbPromise) so all concurrent getDb() callers share one init promise and wait for initSchema to complete. (3) Moved PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL to a standalone runAsync before the execAsync batch.

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling Ã¢â‚¬â€ exceptions would propagate uncaught

bugs.error_message: Rooms assigned on web do not show on housekeeper mobile
bugs.root_cause: HousekeeperBar in web shows both housekeeper and housekeeping_supervisor for assignment, but _ensure_housekeeper only validated role=housekeeper (blocking saves for supervisors), and /my-rooms used require_role("housekeeper") only (returning 403 for supervisors). Mobile silently falls back to empty cache on any API error.
bugs.fix: Changed _ensure_housekeeper to .in_("role", ["housekeeper", "housekeeping_supervisor"]) and changed /my-rooms to require_role("housekeeper", "housekeeping_supervisor")

bugs.error_message: Failed to refresh rooms: [Error: HTTP 400] / no rooms assigned api error: HTTP 400
bugs.root_cause: room_assignments query selects clean_type but has no fallback try/except. If migration 042_room_assignment_clean_type.sql is not applied, PostgREST throws APIError â†’ main.py postgrest_exception_handler returns HTTP 400 with {"error":{"code":...,"message":...}} (no "detail" key) â†’ mobile API client falls through to throw new Error(`HTTP ${status}`) = 'HTTP 400'. Secondary: dnd_flag was missing from my_rooms_select so DND rooms always showed generic subtitle.
bugs.fix: Wrapped room_assignments query in try/except using existing _is_missing_clean_type_column_error fallback pattern (retries without clean_type). Added dnd_flag to my_rooms_select.

bugs.error_message: Task :react-native-screens:configureCMakeDebug[arm64-v8a] FAILED â€” WARNING: A restricted method in java.lang.System has been called
bugs.root_cause: System JDK is JDK 24 which restricts internal java.lang.System methods that React Native's CMake configuration calls. Gradle 8.14.3 enforces this as fatal. JDK 24 also causes 'Unsupported class file major version 68' with older Gradle/Groovy.
bugs.fix: Added org.gradle.java.home=C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft\\jdk-21.0.3.9-hotspot to gradle.properties to pin the Gradle daemon to JDK 21 LTS. Gradle wrapper stays at 8.14.3.

bugs.error_message: WARN Failed to refresh rooms: [Error: HTTP 400] â€” no rooms showing on mobile My Rooms screen
bugs.root_cause: my_rooms_select started with 'id, room_id, ...' but room_status uses room_id as PRIMARY KEY â€” there is no id column. PostgreSQL returned error 42703 (undefined_column), which PostgREST forwarded as an APIError. main.py postgrest_exception_handler maps non-PGRST204 APIErrors to HTTP 400 with body {error:{code,message}}, not {detail:...}, so the mobile client falls back to 'HTTP 400'. Previous fix attempts added clean_type try/except but never removed the invalid id field.
bugs.fix: Removed 'id' from my_rooms_select. Added explicit 'id': room_id mapping in the row-building loop so mobile app's room.id navigation and /rooms/${room.id}/status API calls continue to work.

bugs.fix: Changed IN_PROGRESS: 0, DIRTY: 1, OCCUPIED: 1, PICKUP: 2, CLEAN: 3, INSPECTED: 4, â†’ 0, DIRTY: 1, PICKUP: 2, CLEAN: 3, INSPECTED: 4,

Wrong reference: const should be return
Local dashboard was stuck after the greeting and did not load dashboard sections
bugs.root_cause: apps/web/.env.local points NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL at http://localhost:8003/v1, but the existing Python process on port 8003 accepted connections and timed out on /docs, /openapi.json, and dashboard API calls.
bugs.fix: Stopped the stale 8003 Python process, restarted FastAPI from apps/api on port 8003, restarted the long-lived Next dev server on port 3000, and verified /dashboard renders the housekeeper stats, queue, heads-up, and predictions sections.

bugs.error_message: Jest failed because @/lib/housekeeping/roomWorkflow did not exist when new TDD tests were added
bugs.root_cause: The shared mobile housekeeping room workflow helper was introduced test-first and had not been implemented yet.
bugs.fix: Added apps/mobile/lib/housekeeping/roomWorkflow.ts with action, badge, timing, priority, and Before You Enter warning helpers, then reran the focused Jest test successfully.

bugs.error_message: Full mobile Jest suite failed on stale HousekeeperHome, TasksVariationA, and ReportIssueModal test expectations after current UI contracts changed
bugs.root_cause: Tests expected exact nested text nodes, old placeholder task timeline data, and an obsolete ReportIssueModal description-input/testID/default category flow.
bugs.fix: Updated tests to assert visible current UI behavior: Home uses getAllByText for nested room text, Tasks asserts the empty state for no tasks, and ReportIssueModal fills required title/category/details before submitting online/offline.

bugs.error_message: PowerShell command failed with 'app : The term app is not recognized' when an Expo route path containing (app) was passed to git diff without quotes
bugs.root_cause: PowerShell interpreted parentheses in apps/mobile/app/(app)/... as syntax instead of a literal path segment.
bugs.fix: Reran git diff with the Expo route paths quoted, e.g. git diff --stat -- 'apps/mobile/app/(app)/my-rooms/index.tsx'.
